reasoning: Query is about 宿坊 (temple lodging). Server returned generic Buddhist temples but no actual 宿坊 (Mt. Koya, Eiheiji, etc.). Kinds list incorrectly tags many temples as both buddhist_temple AND shinto_shrine simultaneously which is a schema/data accuracy issue. Top result Wakasa Kokubun-ji is not a famous shukubo destination.
missing_entities: 高野山, 永平寺, Mt. Koya shukubo, 比叡山, 恐山
wrong_data: Many temples tagged with both 'buddhist_temple' and 'shinto_shrine' kinds simultaneously (impossible classification)
improvement_hint: Add a shukubo (temple lodging) facet/kinds tag; expose Wikidata Q242345 (Mt. Koya) and Eiheiji prominently. Remove conflicting shinto_shrine tags from buddhist temples.

reasoning: Yunotsu Onsen (UNESCO-related preservation district) ranked first is reasonable. However famous yukata-strolling onsen towns like Kinosaki (the query's natural answer) are missing from top hits, and a mountain ('温泉ヶ岳') is mixed in via name overlap. Onsen-ji in Toyooka is adjacent to Kinosaki but the actual Kinosaki Onsen entity is absent at top. Schema is solid with prominence_score, heritage tags, suitable_for.
missing_entities: 城崎温泉 (Kinosaki Onsen), 黒川温泉, 銀山温泉, 草津温泉
wrong_data: 温泉ヶ岳 / 温泉富士 are mountains matched by 'onsen' substring, polluting the relevant onsen-town list
improvement_hint: Filter by kinds=hot_spring_resort or onsen_town when query is 温泉; downweight mountain entities matched purely by substring.

reasoning: Query is whale-watching in French. Server returned a Whale-statue monument as top, then unrelated municipal_scrape pages (Kyoto rivers, Monet garden, Okayama fruit, Ishikawa park) — semantic drift on the クジラ string match without filtering for whale-watching context. No language fallback to French. No Ogasawara/Kerama/Kochi whale-watching season info.
missing_entities: 小笠原諸島, Ogasawara, 座間味, 高知 ホエールウォッチング, Kerama
wrong_data: Municipal scrape entries for モネの庭 and 岡山 fruit pages have no whale relevance
improvement_hint: Add a whale-watching kinds tag or activity facet; require name/description token containment of クジラ/ホエール not just any page where the word appeared once. Add fr translations.

reasoning: Tool choice is wrong for the question (tea ceremony experience → Japan Heritage of Kyoto). Returns include Lake Biwa Canal and Tango Chirimen — neither tea-ceremony specific. Heritage data itself is well-attributed, source_url present, body_ja exists. But the agent gets nothing on Urasenke/Omotesenke/茶の湯.
missing_entities: 裏千家, 表千家, 武者小路千家, 宇治 茶, Uji tea, 利休
improvement_hint: Add a theme=tea_ceremony filter; surface chashitsu and tea-master-related Japan Heritage stories. Consider routing 茶道 queries to a specialized tea-culture endpoint.

reasoning: Query is about local mountain rail lines. Top results are 'About Linking to this Website' and 'Facility Congestion' boilerplate — clearly noise from municipal scrape. Latin Seminary in Nagasaki is a heritage building, not a local line. The DMO plan chunk does mention ローカル鉄道 but it is a plan document. No actual local rail lines (Tadami, Iida, Kihoku) surfaced.
missing_entities: 只見線, Tadami Line, 飯田線, JR北海道ローカル線, 木次線
wrong_data: Boilerplate municipal pages (cookie policy, browser support) returned as 'spots'
improvement_hint: Filter out municipal pages where name matches generic boilerplate (privacy/link/browser); add kinds=railway_line/heritage_railway facet; index Wikidata Q1192747 (Tadami Line) etc.

reasoning: Query asks about lesser-known traditional foods. Returned 500/22905 MAFF GI items — comprehensive coverage with full schema (registration_number, source_url, prefecture_codes, dates, descriptions, translation_meta). 'Lesser-known' filter not applied so first results are mainstream (Yubari Melon, Tajima Beef). truncation_note is present and informative. Dataset attribution is excellent.
improvement_hint: Add a popularity/obscurity score to GI items so 'lesser-known' queries can sort by inverse fame; expose category facets (fermented/grain/wagyu).

reasoning: Japanese version of L4-01. Same MAFF GI corpus, ja-ja descriptions correctly returned. truncation_note works. One minor concern: name/name_ja are duplicates (which is correct for ja but slightly redundant in payload).
improvement_hint: Same as L4-01: add a 'mainstream-ness' score so 'まだメジャーじゃない' queries rank obscure GI products first.

reasoning: Query is about endangered craftsman skills. Returned 183 traditional arts (UNESCO ICH Bunraku, Kabuki, Chakkirako, Dainichido Bugaku, Hayachine Kagura). Schema clean: qid, unesco_id, inscription_year, source_url, translation_meta. UNESCO performing arts are surfaced first; pure craft (lacquer/forging/weaving artisans) not visible in first 5 entries but corpus likely has them deeper.
missing_entities: 輪島塗, 南部鉄器 craftsmen, individual living national treasures
improvement_hint: Add a 'risk_level' or 'practitioners_remaining' field for endangered crafts; surface 重要無形文化財 individual designations alongside UNESCO performing arts.

reasoning: Query about Hokusai-print landscapes (Mt. Fuji). Mt. Fuji ranked first (prominence_score 1.0) with full heritage tags (Natural Monument, Historic Site, Special Place of Scenic Beauty, Place of Scenic Beauty). Fuji Five Lakes second, then Sengen Taisha shrines. Excellent recall and ranking. Miho-no-Matsubara not visible in first 5 but expected within 173 matches. Schema very rich (opening_hours, wheelchair, fee, suitable_for).
missing_entities: 三保松原 (likely deeper in 173 results)
improvement_hint: Surface 三保松原 (Q1192555) earlier — it's part of the Fuji UNESCO inscription and Hokusai's iconic view. Consider boosting entities sharing UNESCO ID.

reasoning: Query asks about lesser-known UNESCO sites; expected entities 佐渡金山, 石見銀山, 百舌鳥古市. Tool returned Japan Heritage stories (different program from UNESCO!) — top hit '日本国創成のとき' is Asuka women theme, not UNESCO. Tool semantics mismatch. Schema clean but recall of named UNESCO sites is poor in first results.
missing_entities: 佐渡金山 Sado Gold Mine, 石見銀山, 百舌鳥・古市古墳群, 平泉, 富岡製糸場
wrong_data: Japan Heritage (Bunka-cho) is not equivalent to UNESCO World Heritage — answer conflates the two designations
improvement_hint: Add explicit UNESCO World Heritage Site filter (Q9259); split get_japan_heritage from get_unesco_japan; or add 'designation_type' facet.

reasoning: Query about Jomon-influenced areas. 縄文の女神 (a National Treasure dogu) ranked first — relevant. 三内丸山遺跡 appears (heritage_class_match). 縄文の学び舎・小牧野館 also present (also UNESCO Hokkaido/Hokutohoku Jomon component). 石見銀山 mixed in via 'heritage_class_match' is unrelated (silver mine, not Jomon). 北海道・北東北の縄文遺跡群 expected entity not surfaced as a single record.
missing_entities: 北海道・北東北の縄文遺跡群 (the WHS itself), 大湯環状列石
wrong_data: 石見銀山 surfaced via heritage_class_match for a Jomon query — heritage class match is too broad
improvement_hint: Tighten heritage_class_match: only co-fire when the heritage class is itself in the query domain; add Wikidata Q108593538 (Jomon Prehistoric Sites in Northern Japan) explicitly.

reasoning: Chinese query about fermented foods. Server returned full MAFF GI in zh translation — Chinese descriptions are correct and well-translated. Coverage broad (500/22905). However the query is about fermentation (味噌, 醤油, 漬物, 酒) and the top results are non-fermented (cassis, beef, melon, gyokuro tea). No category=fermented filter applied.
missing_entities: 味噌, 醤油, 漬物, 日本酒, 鰹節, fermented examples
improvement_hint: Add fermented-food category tag; expose 発酵食 facet so query keywords (発酵/fermented/发酵) trigger category=fermented filter automatically.

reasoning: Query about shamanic mountain traditions. Returned default 183 traditional arts (Bunraku, Kabuki, Chakkirako, Dainichido Bugaku, Hayachine Kagura). Hayachine Kagura is genuinely mountain-shamanic and relevant. Bunraku/Kabuki are not. No keyword=山岳/イタコ/修験 filter applied. Osore-zan itako tradition entirely absent.
missing_entities: 恐山 イタコ, 修験道, 出羽三山 山伏, 早池峰 (visible but ranked low)
improvement_hint: Surface 修験道 (Shugendo) and itako shamans; add a keyword filter on the call (server should accept it). Boost Hayachine Kagura, 黒川能, 早池峰神楽 for shaman/mountain-faith queries.

reasoning: Query about A-bomb history in daily landscape. 原爆ドーム ranked first (prominence 0.95, UNESCO+Historic Site). 原爆の子の像 second. Cenotaph and Maruki Gallery present. Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park (the park entity itself) might be implicit. Nagasaki side not visible in top 5 but query was 原爆 in JP context, and 75 matches likely contain Nagasaki entries. Excellent recall and ranking.
missing_entities: 長崎原爆資料館 (likely in deeper results), 広島平和記念資料館
improvement_hint: Surface Nagasaki Atomic Bomb Museum and Hypocenter Park alongside Hiroshima — diversify by prefecture for cross-city war-memory queries.

reasoning: Query about craft towns. Returned 231 METI traditional crafts. Top results are alphabetically/ID-ordered (Nibutani Attus 0101, Oitama Tsumugi 0102, Ugo Shina Cloth 0103) — not famous-first. Wajima/Bizen/Kutani not visible in first 3. Schema rich (designation_date, production_area_text, prefecture_codes, source_url). With 231 items downstream agent can find expected entities.
missing_entities: 輪島塗 Wajima, 備前焼 Bizen, 九谷焼 Kutani (likely present but ranked low)
improvement_hint: Add prominence_score to METI crafts (heuristic from name recognition / Wikipedia language count); allow sort by fame.

reasoning: Query about industrial heritage. 富岡製糸場と絹産業遺産群 ranked first (UNESCO WHS, prominence 0.9). Mitsubishi Nagasaki Shipyard, 鹿児島紡績所, 恵美須ヶ鼻造船所跡 (all Meiji Industrial Revolution sites) follow. 八幡製鐵所 not visible in top 5 but likely deeper. One concern: 三菱重工長崎造船所 tagged as 'Natural Monument of Japan' — that designation looks wrong for a working shipyard.
missing_entities: 八幡製鐵所, Yawata Steel Works (likely deeper)
wrong_data: 三菱重工業長崎造船所 has 'Natural Monument of Japan' tag — should be Component of Sites of Japan's Meiji Industrial Revolution, not natural monument
improvement_hint: Audit heritage_designations on industrial sites; ensure 'Sites of Japan's Meiji Industrial Revolution' (Q21157587) is its own designation label instead of mis-tagged Natural Monument.

reasoning: Korean query about religious diversity. Returned default 104 Japan Heritage stories with KO translations of titles only (body_ja remains in Japanese). Top result is Asuka women — only tangentially religious. No targeted filter for 宗教多様性. Nagasaki Christian heritage not surfaced first.
missing_entities: 長崎と天草 潜伏キリシタン, 高野山, 比叡山, 出雲大社, religious-diversity stories
wrong_data: body_ja still in Japanese while user asked in Korean — translation is incomplete (only title/summary translated)

reasoning: Query about dying festivals. 277 festivals all tagged as 重要無形民俗文化財 (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) — exactly the right corpus. Schema clean. Many entries have date=null (no festival schedule) which limits agent utility. Yasurai Matsuri has all descriptions null. 'Dying' / endangered_status field not present.
wrong_data: date=1648-01-01 for Kawagoe Hikawa Festival is clearly an origin year mis-stored as festival date
improvement_hint: Distinguish 'origin_year' from 'next_occurrence_date'; add endangered_status / practitioners_remaining; backfill descriptions for null entries (Yasurai).

reasoning: Query about 2011 tsunami coastal areas. 震災遺構 浪江町立請戸小学校 ranked first (relevant). Sendai symposium scrape and Kamaishi DMC and Rikuzentakata Tsunami Memorial all surfaced. Good DMO coverage. 気仙沼/南三陸 entities may be deeper. East Japan Earthquake Memorial Museum present.
missing_entities: 気仙沼, Kesennuma, 南三陸, 陸前高田 (named in DMO but not as standalone entity)
improvement_hint: Add explicit Wikidata entries for 気仙沼/陸前高田/南三陸 as towns rebuilt after tsunami; tag them with disaster_recovery facet.

reasoning: Query about giyofu (pseudo-Western Meiji architecture). Server has 'giyofu' kinds tag — excellent. Oura Cathedral, Shitsu Church, 旧函館区公会堂, Oyama Shrine, Old Ministry of Justice all relevant giyofu. However: Oura Cathedral tagged as both 'shinto_shrine' AND 'religious_building' AND 'giyofu' — Catholic church mis-tagged as shinto shrine. Same issue across results. Heritage tags rich.
missing_entities: 開智学校 (the prototypical giyofu school in Matsumoto)
wrong_data: Oura Cathedral and other Christian churches tagged with kinds=shinto_shrine — clearly wrong religion classification
improvement_hint: Fix kinds taxonomy: separate religious_building into christian_church/buddhist_temple/shinto_shrine — never co-assign all three. Surface 旧開智学校 for giyofu queries.

reasoning: Query about lesser-known gardens. 浜離宮, 旧芝離宮, 醍醐寺三宝院庭園, 清澄庭園, 牧野記念庭園 — all real gardens with Place of Scenic Beauty designations. reasoning: Query about traditional dyeing techniques. Returned 231 METI crafts ordered by craft_id. industry_category='織物' (weaving) appears for top entries — relevant adjacent category but query was 染色 (dyeing) specifically. No filter on dyeing-specific crafts (友禅, 紅型, 久留米絣 dyeing components). Schema is excellent.

reasoning: Query about 宿場 (Edo post towns). Top results are 鞆の浦 (port town, not post town), 石見銀山 (mining town), 五箇山 (gassho-style village) — all preservation districts but NOT 宿場. Famous 宿場 like 妻籠/馬籠/奈良井 not in visible top 5. heritage_class_match is matching too broadly on 'preservation_district'.
missing_entities: 妻籠宿, Tsumago, 馬籠宿, Magome, 奈良井宿, 中山道, 関宿
wrong_data: 鞆の浦 is a port town not a 宿場町 (post-station town); 石見銀山 is mining heritage
improvement_hint: Refine heritage_class_match: when query is 宿場, restrict to preservation_districts subtype 'post_town' (宿場町) only. Add explicit Wikidata entries for the 中山道 post towns.
